children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The President, acting through the Administrator, in coordination with State, local, and tribal governments, shall establish and conduct a pilot program. The pilot program shall be designed to make better use of existing rental housing, located in areas covered by a major disaster declaration, in order to provide timely and cost-effective temporary housing assistance to individuals and households eligible for assistance under where alternative housing options are less available or less cost-effective.section 5174 of title 42

children.label: i
children.text: enter into lease agreements with owners of multi-family rental property located in areas covered by a major disaster declaration to house individuals and households eligible for assistance under ;section 5174 of title 42

children.label: ii
children.text: make improvements to properties under such lease agreements;

children.label: iii
children.text: use the pilot program where the program is cost effective in that the cost to the Government for the lease agreements is in proportion to the savings to the Government by not providing alternative housing; and

children.label: iv
children.text: limit repairs to those required to ensure that the housing units shall meet Federal housing quality standards.

children.label: i
children.text: shall be deducted from the value of the lease agreement; and

children.label: ii
children.text: may not exceed the value of the lease agreement.

children.heading: Consultation
children.label: 3 Consultation
children.text: In administering the pilot program under this section, the Administrator may consult with State, local, and tribal governments.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Not later than , the Administrator shall submit to the appropriate committees of Congress a report regarding the effectiveness of the pilot program.March 31, 2009

children.label: i
children.text: an assessment of the effectiveness of the pilot program under this section, including an assessment of cost-savings to the Federal Government and any benefits to individuals and households eligible for assistance under under the pilot program;section 5174 of title 42

children.label: ii
children.text: findings and conclusions of the Administrator with respect to the pilot program;

children.label: iii
children.text: an assessment of additional authorities needed to aid the Agency in its mission of providing disaster housing assistance to individuals and households eligible for assistance under , either under the pilot program under this section or other potential housing programs; andsection 5174 of title 42

children.label: iv
children.text: any recommendations of the Administrator for additional authority to continue or make permanent the pilot program.

children.heading: Pilot program project approval
children.label: b Pilot program project approval
children.text: The Administrator shall not approve a project under the pilot program after .December 31, 2008
